hero.title: 4 Days in Varanasi.
hero.titleAccent: The Sacred City, Sarnath and Beyond.
hero.intro: An extra day to go deeper into the oldest living city. Beyond the sunrise boats and the Ganga Aarti, this four-day journey takes in Sarnath, the temples, the silk weavers and the quieter corners of sacred Varanasi.

breadcrumb.label: 4 Days Best of Varanasi: Most Important Places

hero.heroImageAlt: Varanasi ghats sunrise boat on the Ganges

overview.heading: 4 Days Best of Varanasi: Most Important Places: The Full Picture
body: Four days lets Varanasi reveal more of itself. Alongside the essential sunrise boat ride and the evening Ganga Aarti, you have time for the Buddhist site of Sarnath, the great temples, the labyrinth of old lanes, the silk weavers, and the quieter ghats away from the crowds.

This 4 day Best of Varanasi package from Malaysia is private throughout, with your own vehicle and driver guide and both sunrise and sunset boat rides included. The extra day allows for a more relaxed, contemplative experience of the spiritual capital, with optional Pind Daan prayers and time to absorb the city's intensity.

highlights.heading: Why Travellers from Malaysia Love This Trip

items.title: Sunrise Boat Ride & Ganga Aarti
items.body: The two essential Varanasi experiences: drifting along the ghats at first light, and the spectacular evening Ganga Aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat, with fire, bells and chanting.
items.imageAlt: Varanasi sunrise boat and Ganga Aarti

items.title: Sarnath
items.body: A short drive from the city, Sarnath is where the Buddha gave his first sermon. Its stupas, ruins and museum make a peaceful, important counterpoint to the intensity of the ghats.

items.title: Temples, Lanes & Silk
items.body: Kashi Vishwanath and the city's great temples, the maze of old lanes, and the famous Banarasi silk weavers, all explored at a relaxed pace with your guide.

days.title: Arrive Varanasi
days.description: Met at the airport and transferred to your hotel. Evening Ganga Aarti at Dashashwamedh Ghat from a boat.
suggestedActivities: Evening Ganga Aarti from a boat

days.title: Sunrise Boat, Temples & Sarnath
days.description: Sunrise boat ride, temples, then the Buddhist site of Sarnath.

days.title: Ghats, Silk & Quiet Corners

days.title: Depart

whyBook.heading: Varanasi in Depth, With the Right Guidance
whyBook.body: Varanasi rewards time and understanding. The extra day and a knowledgeable guide turn the intensity into something profound. We handle the boats, the timings and the lanes. Dhesu has arranged spiritual India journeys since 1988.

sections.heading: Best of Varanasi Tour from Malaysia
sections.body:
This 4 day package gives Varanasi the time it deserves. Beyond the sunrise boat ride and the Ganga Aarti, you explore Sarnath, the temples, the silk weavers and the quieter corners of the sacred city.

The extra day and a private guide mean a more relaxed, contemplative experience, with both sunrise and sunset boat rides included.

Varanasi pairs naturally with Ayodhya and Prayagraj, covered in our 5 day and 6 day spiritual circuit packages.

sections.heading: Sarnath and the Buddha
sections.body:
Just outside Varanasi, Sarnath is one of the four great Buddhist pilgrimage sites, where the Buddha gave his first sermon after attaining enlightenment.

The Dhamek Stupa, the ruins of ancient monasteries and the excellent archaeological museum make it a peaceful, meaningful half-day, and a striking contrast to the intensity of the Varanasi ghats.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for India?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders apply for an India e-Visa online before departure. The tourist e-Visa covers tourism and short visits and is valid for 30 days from entry. The application is on the official India e-Visa portal, and Dhesu walks you through it when you book. Visa fees are not included in the package price.

items.question: Which airport does this trip use?
items.answer: This is a land-only package: hotels, transfers, tours and guide are included, but international flights are not. Most Malaysian travellers fly from Kuala Lumpur International Airport (KLIA) to Lal Bahadur Shastri International Airport, Varanasi (VNS). We can recommend suitable flights and timings on request.

items.question: What does the 4 day version add over the 3 day?
items.answer: The extra day allows for a deeper, more relaxed experience: more time at Sarnath, the temples, the silk weavers and the quieter ghats, plus the option of Pind Daan prayers, rather than rushing the highlights.

items.question: Are both boat rides included?
items.answer: Yes. This package includes both the sunrise boat ride along the ghats and an evening boat for the Ganga Aarti, the two essential Varanasi experiences.

items.question: Can I arrange Pind Daan or special prayers?
items.answer: Yes. Many visitors come to Varanasi for Pind Daan and ancestral rites. We can arrange these with experienced pandits. Please let us know when you book so we can prepare.
